cot_prefix: Okay, let's try to figure out the answer to this question. So, the question is asking what Mr. Williams was doing when he was stopped by the policeman. The choices are A to D. First, I need to recall the details from the story. Mr. Williams was driving home and came to a crossroad with a "SLOW" sign. He slowed down, looked both ways, and then drove across without stopping completely. Then he heard a police whistle, pulled over, and the policeman said he didn't stop at the crossing. Mr. Williams argued that the sign said "SLOW" not "STOP," and he did slow down. The policeman then realized he was in the wrong street. The key here is the sequence of events. The officer stopped him because he didn't stop at the crossing. But Mr. Williams had a "SLOW" sign, so he didn't have to stop, just slow down. The officer later realized his mistake. So when the policeman approached him, what was Mr. Williams doing? The question is about the time he was stopped. The options are: A) driving at high speed B) driving onto the main road C) going to stop his car D) driving slowly From the story, Mr. Williams slowed down when he approached the main road. He didn't stop but continued after slowing. When the police whistle blew, he pulled over. At the moment he was stopped, he was driving onto the main road after slowing down. But wait, when he was stopped by the policeman, was he still on the main road or in the process of crossing? Wait, the story says he "then drove across without stopping completely." So he had already slowed and crossed the main road. Then he heard the whistle while driving, pulled over. So maybe the timing is when he was on the main road. Alternatively, when was he stopped?
